summary: Full backend + frontend end-to-end testing of NNE Data Analyst assessment app. All 18 backend pytest cases passed (sets, questions, sessions, SQL/Python sandbox, AI-graded submit with idempotency, history/results). Full UI happy-path (Landing -> Test with SQL+Python execution -> Submit -> Results -> History) worked flawlessly. AI grader awarded 5/5 for correct SQL answer and produced meaningful feedback in ~15-20s.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L153: after asyncio.wait_for timeout, proc.kill() is called but proc.communicate() is not awaited again which can leave a zombie briefly; minor - not blocking.

server.py L142: BLOCKED_PY regex is a simple word-boundary scan; a candidate could still bypass with getattr(__builtins__, 'eval')(...) or file writes via open(). Acceptable for internal test tool but not sandbox-safe for public exposure.
server.py L224: min(5, max(0, float(item['score']))) silently clamps invalid grader scores instead of logging; consider logging out-of-range values.
Landing.js L119: fallback sets are hardcoded if /api/sets fails - candidates could start with a set that the backend doesn't recognise. Should show error state instead.
No auth on any endpoint - anyone with the URL can list sessions/results and submit answers. Acceptable per PRD ('no auth in app') but worth documenting.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in the app
seed_data_creation: Created 2 sessions during testing (TEST_Candidate Set A, TEST_Submit Set A, TEST_UI_Candidate Set A). 2 new graded results now in history.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend + frontend both fully functional. AI grading via Emergent Claude Sonnet 4.6 takes ~15-20s for 20 questions (parallel batches of 5). Submit endpoint requires timeout >= 60s (240s recommended). History accumulates test entries; use TEST_ prefix for candidate_name to identify test data. No cleanup endpoint exists so TEST_ entries remain in MongoDB.
